session_hint: the mobile layout breakage on the checkout page investigation
question: What was the root cause of the layout breaking on mobile devices on the checkout page?
turns: Mobile users seeing broken layout on the checkout page — everything is tiny and unscrollable. Desktop looks fine. The root cause is already clear: the checkout page template is missing the viewport meta tag. Mobile browsers fall back to a default 980px layout width and then scale the page down to fit, making all content unreadably small.

Double-checked media query ordering in our main stylesheet anyway — all breakpoints are correct and @media (max-width: 768px) rules are present with the right specificity. Not being overridden by anything.
Looked at flex vs grid usage on the checkout page — it uses a mix of both but both are well-supported on all mobile browsers we target. Layout model is not the cause.

Investigated font-size inheritance: a few rem values look like they could cascade oddly, but reducing them doesn't change the zoomed-out viewport behavior. Font sizes are not causing the layout collapse.
Confirmed the fix: adding <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1'> to the checkout template makes the layout render correctly at all tested screen sizes immediately.

Traced the omission: the checkout page was built two weeks ago from a new template that was created from scratch rather than extending the base layout template. All other pages use the base template which already has the viewport tag.
Media queries, flex/grid, and font-size inheritance all verified as correct — none are contributing to the issue.
Deployed the viewport meta tag fix. Mobile checkout layout renders correctly on Android and iOS.
Added a Playwright test that checks for the viewport meta tag on every page in our sitemap. Will catch any future template that omits it.
